content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "A Lot of Games". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/contest/455/problem/B
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer n, which is the number of games. Then, n lines follow, each containing two integers, a and b, which represent the scores of the two players in a game.
content.outputFormat: Output the number of games won by the first player, the number of games won by the second player, and the number of draws.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Compare the scores of each game and count the number of wins, losses, and draws.

code.Python3:
n = int(input())
a, b = 0, 0
win1, win2, draw = 0, 0, 0
for _ in range(n):
    a, b = map(int, input().split())
    if a > b: win1 += 1
    elif a < b: win2 += 1
    else: draw += 1
print(win1, win2, draw)
